danger_signals.visual_form: Evil pumpkin head spawns when player takes too long to complete level
danger_signals.meaning: Player is failing to progress efficiently; danger escalates with inaction
Direct threat to player health; loss of life on contact

danger_signals.visual_form: Ethereal ghost entities emerge from pumpkin head after prolonged level time
danger_signals.meaning: Unavoidable threat; cannot be killed or stunned; signals critical time pressure failure

danger_signals.visual_form: Attacks fired by monsters toward player character
danger_signals.meaning: Ranged damage source; loss of life and penalty on hit

danger_signals.visual_form: Character animation/behavior change after being hit
danger_signals.meaning: Cumulative damage penalty; weakened combat capability until next level

safety_signals.visual_form: Enemy covered completely in snow, transformed into immobile ball
safety_signals.meaning: Threat neutralized; enemy cannot move or attack

safety_signals.visual_form: Enemy partially covered in snow with visible animation of struggling
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary threat reduction; enemy immobilized but not eliminated

safety_signals.visual_form: Colored bottles dropping from defeated enemies (red, blue, yellow, green)
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary power enhancement; increases offensive or defensive capability

safety_signals.visual_form: All enemies eliminated; visual clearing of level
safety_signals.meaning: Safety restored; progression to next stage

safety_signals.visual_form: Character teleports back to beginning location after being downed
safety_signals.meaning: Second chance; vulnerability window during respawn but goal state unchanged

faction_indicators.color_identity: Nick: blue overalls; Tom: red overalls
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Cursed into snowman form by King Scorch (NES version narrative)

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Diverse monster sprite designs; distinct from player snowmen
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Various monster types with different behaviors and appearances

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Represents authoritarian threat; cause of player curse and princess captivity
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: NES version: ultimate boss at end of stage progression; represents tyranny

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Objective markers; targets of rescue mission
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Goal entities; motivation for player progression through 50 stages

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Standard enemy sprites; normal level layout; standard color palette
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies patrol and attack normally; no time pressure threats visible

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Evil pumpkin head appears visually in level space
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Pumpkin head hunts player; invincible but can be stunned with snowballs; spawns ghosts

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Ghost entities visible; ethereal sprite design contrasting normal enemies
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Ghosts travel freely, seek player actively; cannot be killed or stunned; only avoidance possible

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Unique boss sprite design distinct from standard enemies
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Boss must be defeated before progression; escalated difficulty challenge

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Snow visible on enemy sprite; reduced animation frame rate or idle state
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy cannot move; vulnerable to knockback or chain reactions; temporary stun state

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Enemy sprite replaced with white spherical snowball object
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Becomes physics object; can be kicked/rolled; bounces off walls; eliminates other enemies on contact

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Character sprite or particle effects change; visual indicator of enhancement
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Increased speed, snow volume, throwing distance, or flight capability; limited duration buff

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Character animation shows damage reaction; possible visual reduction in size or glow
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Firepower and speed reset to default; life counter decreases; temporary invincibility window

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Money/bonus items fall from top of screen
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Scoring bonus triggered; visual reward for efficient play

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: All lives exhausted; game state transition screen
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player cannot continue without inserting credits (arcade) or game reset

environmental_storytelling.example: Red potion = speed boost; blue = snow volume; yellow = throw distance; green = flight. Visual color immediately communicates power-up function without UI text.
Nick wears blue overalls, Tom wears red. Visual distinction allows quick player identification in multiplayer and establishes character identity in intro/narrative.
Early stages show standard monsters; time pressure introduces pumpkin head; critical failure spawns ghosts. Visual threat complexity communicates stage progression and time state.
Every 10th stage features visually distinct boss sprite, signaling story beats and progression milestones without dialogue.

environmental_storytelling.technique: Snowball mechanic as world state indicator
environmental_storytelling.example: Enemies transition from mobile threat → partially stunned (visual snow coverage) → immobile snowball (physics object). Visual transformation shows player agency and threat neutralization.

environmental_storytelling.technique: Pumpkin head as time mechanic visualization
environmental_storytelling.example: Pumpkin head is tangible visual manifestation of abstract time pressure, making invisible timer visible and threatening.

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Can throw snow in arc trajectory; movement across platforms; responsive to input
Increased movement speed; faster response time
Snow spray volume increased; easier enemy coverage; wider attack pattern
Snow throw distance increased; longer projectile range
Inflates like balloon; flight capability; can move freely across screen; knockdown attack on enemies
Speed reduced to baseline; firepower reduced to baseline; temporary invincibility flash
Patrol stage; attack player on proximity; follow programmed AI patterns
Frozen in place; cannot move or attack; animated struggling to shake off snow; vulnerable to knockback
No autonomous movement; becomes physics object; rolls/bounces on impact; chain-reacts with other snowballs
Hunts player actively; invincible to normal attacks; can be stunned and repositioned via snowballs; threatens level completion
Travel freely through level terrain; seek player; cannot be stunned or killed; only avoidance possible; forces evasion gameplay
Elevated difficulty; must be defeated to progress; likely enhanced damage output or armor vs. standard enemies

notes: Snow Bros. NES uses visual state transformation as primary danger/safety signaling system. Enemy transformation from mobile threat → partially frozen → snowball weapon creates cascading visual feedback of player agency. Color-coded potions eliminate need for text-based UI explanations in era-appropriate manner. Time pressure mechanic is uniquely visualized through pumpkin head and ghost spawning, making abstract timer into tangible visual threat. Multiplayer color differentiation (blue/red overalls) establishes character identity without narrative exposition. Boss encounter placement (every 10th stage) creates rhythmic visual story beats. Political narrative (King Scorch tyranny, princess rescue) is minimal in visual presentation but establishes rescue-quest framework.
